stemHtml:
A twin-engine aircraft has three fuel tanks with the following capacity and fuel on board at take-off. The left fuel tank feeds the left engine and the right fuel tank feeds the right engine. A fuel pump connected to the centre tank feeds the left and right fuel tanks with a rate of 115 kg/hr. The fuel consumption is 125 kg/hr per engine, and after 37 minutes of flight, the fuel pump feeding the left and right tanks fails. What is the aircraft's remaining endurance?

Left fuel tank capacity: 205 kg
Right fuel tank capacity: 205 kg
Centre fuel tank capacity: 600 kg
Total fuel on board: 600 kg
